Summary: Duane Bender introduced these guys.

Hello Eliot,
 
I hope things are well with you.
 
I am e-troducing you to Alex and Amit. They are working for MARS innovation down at SickKids on a product that embeds HL7v2.6 interfaces. They have some questions about conformance profiles and sample messages in v2.6. I was hoping that you could give them some useful pointers as my knowledge of v2.6 is quite limited as you know.
 
Thanks and kind regards,
 
-duane
Summary: Have met with them a couple times at RSNA

Summary: Gautam the CEO of gingerCube is a veteran entrepreneur with three previous startups under his belt. He totally gets the concept of less is more. His last start up in the late 90's in India had over 175 employees and it got really crazy. He swore that his next company would have no more than 10 employees maximum and he intends to stick to that plan.

His latest startup gingerCube is the complete embodiment of that philosophy. This is truly a post-PC, cloud based play.

This company is ahead of the curve. It is an example of the lightweight start up. The company has a simple solution for physicians that need to capture ICD/CPT® codes via either web, iPad or iPhone. Dr John Thomas an Orthopaedic Surgeon in Dallas, TX explains the benefits.:

"gingerCube helps me streamline my patient billing. I can code procedures on my iPhone while I am with the patient and the ICD/CPT codes are immediately available for my assistant to file with the insurance company. This means I can avoid many of the issues I used to have with paper notes stuffed in my pocket and crazy stacks of bills with hastily scribbled notes for my assistant to interpret. It means I am recovering all missed billing opportunities and getting insurance payments faster."

gingerCube accomplishes this without putting any burden of maintaining physical infrastructure on the customer, or on gingerCube itself. This company has no physical infrastructure.

In the backend the company uses the Heroku cloud platform. Heroku was recently acquired by SalesForce.com. All the action is done with Ruby on Rails using modern web service standards, JSON and AJAX.

The front end only supports the web and post PC devices like iOS. The company therefore completely bypasses all the traditional problems of deploying and supporting software on the PC platform.

The one complication is how to integrate.

Many of Gautam's physician customers are specialists who work part time in private practice, and also at a partner hospital. As a result there is a need to integrate data from Gautam's solution with their partner hospital or private practice.

This is a challenge for Gautam. He does not want the overhead of a large team of engineers working on system integration. He also knows that outsourcing will not deliver the quality of integration that his customers demand.

This is what makes the Iguana Translator a good solution for him. He gets the idea that this is not a tool that pretends to make it easy for idiots to do HL7. Instead it's a way to make engineers productive at doing high quality integrations quickly. In Gautam's own words:

"The concept of the Iguana Translator is clever. It's unlike any environment that I have worked with before. The manner in which you can visually see how the data is flowing through and being manipulated in real time by the code is astonishing. You can see how much attention to detail has been put in. It's a good solution to the integration problem. iNTERFACEWARE has fresh perspective of what integration is about."

The Iguana Translator is an elegant solution for Gautam's company. Because it is web based and installs onto a variety of platforms the product works well within Amazon's EC2 cloud. gingerCube talks to Iguana via standard web service APIs and Iguana acts as the universal plug into HL7 and different formats used by other systems.

The general conundrum of HL7 is all about shoving a square peg into a round hole. A modern application like gingerCube will always have a more complete set of data than most older hospital systems.
 
For instance with phone numbers an older mainframe system might only have fields for Home Phone and Business Phone. So to add additional contact information, it has to be squeezed into a plain text field or perhaps use a URL to link back to Gautam's application through the cloud etc.

This is what distinguishes the Iguana Translator - it makes the string munching required for integration highly visible because it is integrated into a single user interface for both mapping and scripting. 
 
Historical Notes

At iNTERFACEWARE we have worked with various mobile integration solutions. One company that used Chameleon for integration back around 2003 was Mercury MD. It was one of the first mobile portals to integrate all the HL7 feeds onto a mobile device. They were later acquired by the Thompson Group.

It is interesting comparing the landscape back then, to now.

Back then Palm OS was king of the hill in mobile computing. Windows was encroaching, so Mercury MD spent a lot of time and money building a cross platform library in C++ to write applications to run on both platforms. That all seems irrelevant now.

At the time most hospitals did not have Wifi networks and Palm and other devices were not connected to mobile 3G networks. As a result the Mercury MD solution required doctors to use physical syncing stations. The way they described it doctors could do this during their morning coffee. Mercury MD also had to install Dell backend servers to support their solution.

Both solutions address a similar problem from a user perspective. The simplicity of Gautam's solution is just a sign of how far technology has advanced to allow a much more efficient solution. From a doctor's perspective it is much easier to have direct access on the iPhone. For the IT department the new cloud based solution needs far less resources to implement.

Incidentally we still OEM Chameleon to Patient Keeper today - we have not moved them on to Iguana yet. Have to introduce them to the Brave New World soon.

Some More Technical Details

If you are curious, we are moving more into the cloud ourselves.

I had a brief look at cloud technologies in December and figured it would be very easy to get lost in the weeds in terms of choices of how to do it - one can easily lose sight of what problem you are trying to solve, and end up losing two years redoing everything. That was the last thing we needed with us trying to focus on getting the Iguana Translator production ready for this year.

From what I see there could be a short and a long term strategy to get ourselves into the cloud. Over a one or two year time frame we could:
 * Use fossil to take all our configuration information and distributed it across a few synced up repositories
 * Alter the back end queuing to sit on top of a no SQL foundation like Couch DB or something 
Then we would have a fully cloud based system, this would be awesome for the challenges associated with high data transaction rates, as faced by the likes of Pharmacy One.
 
Most customers would still be fine with a simple hosted solution. It costs roughly $85 per month to maintain an Amazon EC2 instance to host Iguana. That is not bad at all in the big scheme of things.
 
If you are curious checkout out APIs for dealing with XML and HTTPS.

Other platforms pretend mapping is the main problem and scripting is the frilly bit. To me it feels like where we came from rather than where we are today - the meat of problem is really in the scripting side of interfaces.
 
Mirth is good in that it is a scripting based engine. But lacks the visibility and ease of use that Iguana supplies with its annotations and data driven auto completion.
 
If you find Gautam's profile interesting you might also want to look at Matt.

Summary: Data Integration Project Manager - LionShare Marketing, Lenexa, KS We are searching for an experienced and talented Data Integration Project Manager to become an integral part of our team. This person will work directly with the IT department to identify and secure the data needed to integrate into our systems and perform the integration and data management for various types of client projects. Healthcare data experience and Bachelor’s degree are strongly preferred. We attract candidates who desire to work and flourish in a non‐traditional, fast paced and candid work environment. You will enjoy an environment where ideas flow freely and energy abounds. Responsibilities:  Work collaboratively with management and business partners to identify, propose and coordinate CRM/BI application initiatives.  Manage relationships with CRM/BI group, technology vendors and application developers.  Act as systems specialist for CRM/BI applications.  Perform ad‐hoc analysis and reporting for the CRM/BI group, as needed.  Perform Quality Assurance on all projects and tasks.  Determining priorities, and providing pre and post production support.  Knowledge and understanding of industry leading database marketing software toolsets. Requirements:  Have at least 4+ years of project management and data integration experience, managing multiple projects in a fast‐paced environment.  Have experience in most of the following Database, CRM, and BI systems such as Alterian, Oracle, Cognos, Brio, E‐Piphany, Aprimo, Unica, etc.  Must be fluent in encryption and FTP.  Knowledge of campaign management systems, data mining database management systems, data warehousing, ETL tools, query/reporting languages and tools, OLAP and data mining.  Must possess excellent interpersonal and customer service skills.  Have experience interacting daily with clients and senior management.  Must be proficient at coordinating, developing and documenting Don’t just send us your resume; also tell us why you are the missing piece of our team by contacting Scott Guest at success@lionsharemarketing.com. EOE

Summary: One Medical Passport uses a “patient-friendly” web-based portal to gather required medical information from patients scheduled for surgery. By answering a comprehensive series of questions online from home, patients are guided through the process of submitting a complete medical history. One Medical Passport then automatically processes the information into different downloadable report formats that meet the unique information needs of the nurses, anesthesiologists and surgeons who will care for the patient. This process allows the medical history to be simply verified with the patient, rather than obtained “de novo” multiple times, which reduces the time necessary to complete a preoperative assessment to a fraction of the customary time.

Since Cymetrix’s inception in 2001 by a team of industry innovators, our objective has remained constant—unlock our customers’ hidden financial opportunities while advancing them to the next level of performance.

Our solutions have the flexibility to accommodate the specific needs of each customer and are designed to achieve high revenue cycle performance with a seamless process integrating Patient Access, Health Information Management and Patient Financial Services.

At Cymetrix, we measure ourselves by your achievement—that’s why we work to build a partnership with our customers based on performance and delivering measurable results that ensure our shared growth.

Summary: MOS specializes in the optimization of the reimbursement process through state-of-the-art information technologies, highly trained and skilled staff members, and customized workflow processes.
 
Our mission is to run an efficient and productive billing department with integrity, honesty and strong ethical values so our clients can concentrate on their patients — while being confident that their financial future is secure.

We understand the never-ending stream of challenges you face due to the constant changes in regulations and guidelines affecting the medical field:
Increasingly complex coding
Negotiating and enforcing contracts
Compliance and associated risk
Cash flows affected by declining reimbursements
Decreasing productivity; increased administrative requirements with few resources
 
Since 1985, we have been dedicated to providing quality service to our local and national clients through longstanding healthcare billing and accounts receivable agreements. We have a strong commitment to our vision of being different from every other healthcare billing and accounts receivable management company. With our knowledge, experience and dedication, we guarantee to provide a needed service to secure the financial future of our clients — ultimately becoming a great asset to our clients.

Summary: Management team: http://www.aspenadvisors.net/about/leadership-team Clients engage Aspen Advisors because they know we approach every challenge without preconceived solutions, and we bring operational and healthcare IT expertise to optimize the value of their information technology investments. We understand both health plan and health delivery operations and technology — and more importantly — the intersections and synergies between these two facets of the healthcare industry. Established in 2006, we've grown significantly year-over-year and were named a 2010 "Up and Comer" by Healthcare Informatics. Our hallmarks are top quality service and satisfied clients. We are: DIVERSE. We are a rich mix of respected healthcare technology veterans and rising stars, united by high expectations and a complete commitment to the industry. We bring our varied histories, perspectives, and broad skill-sets to each assignment and deftly collaborate to bring the best ideas forward. DOWN TO EARTH. We are cordial, collegial, and accessible, with no strict hierarchies or prima donnas. We won’t bury you in MBA-speak or bore you with consultant jargon. Instead, we tell it like it is and are always ready to roll up our sleeves and get to work. DEDICATED. We are the best of the best, who love what we do and put our full skills and selves into our work. We take pride in maintaining our impeccable reputation and are committed to ethics, integrity, and 100% client satisfaction. For us, it’s not just business — it’s personal. DISCERNING. We are selective about our client engagements and the talent we bring on board. Our goal is to work with and for industry leaders who are using healthcare technology to foster innovation and make meaningful change. We are seasoned experts who seek the most challenging projects: the ones that will benefit from our unique blend of ingenuity and consulting expertise. DYNAMIC. We are continually growing and evolving — as individuals and as a group. We are nimble, versatile, and resourceful. We bring passion to every engagement, and our mantra is simple: to improve healthcare one project at a time.

Summary: Founded in 1993, ScImage, Inc. is a privately held company headquartered in Los Altos, California. ScImage is a global leader in the development of advanced medical imaging and workflow solutions for radiology, cardiology, orthopedics, obstetrics and electronic medical record content management. As a true enterprise PACS company, ScImage's mission is to provide a shared infrastructure solution for all imaging intensive departments throughout the healthcare enterprise. At the center of ScImage's technology is PICOM, a web service technology that works independent of data types, modalities and departments to seamlessly deliver multi-modality image and information management, multi-PACS archiving, Cloud PACS services, offsite disaster recovery and telemedicine functionality. For more information about ScImage and the PICOM family of products, visit their website at http://www.scimage.com or call 1-866-SCIMAGE. The company’s “PICOM” trademark denotes Patient Information and Communication in Medicine, which expands on the DICOM concept of Digital Imaging and Communication in Medicine.

Summary: About AliveCor Our Heart Monitor is now cleared and available for pre-order to U.S. medical professionals at AliveCor.com. We want everyone to have their health at their fingertips. AliveCor, Inc., a privately held company headquartered in San Francisco, CA, wants everyone to have their health at their fingertips. That's why we make the vital health signs of people (and animals) around the globe easily visible and actionable, at exceptionally low cost. This improves everything from public health to the lives and possibilities of people all over the world. AliveCor has developed a clinical-quality, low-cost mobile ECG device, which enables patients to monitor their heart health anywhere, at any time, and provides physicians with an additional heart health assessment tool. AliveCor’s Heart Monitor for iPhone is initially intended for use by licensed U.S. medical professionals to record, display, store, transfer, and evaluate singlechannel electrocardiogram (ECG) rhythms. The company’s mobile ECG device has been used in clinical studies at the University of Oklahoma Health Sciences Center and the USC Center for Body Computing and abstracts were presented in 2012 at ACC, HRS, and AHA showing that the Heart Monitor demonstrated usability, accuracy, and the ability to be used in community screening. For more information, please visit http://www.alivecor.com or e-mail info@alivecor.com. Source: http://www.linkedin.com/company/alivecor

Summary: L&T Integrated Engineering Services (IES) is a Strategic Business Unit of Larsen & Toubro, a USD 13+ Billion Indian Engineering Conglomerate. 

Acknowledged as one of the fastest growing engineering services provider out of India,we are headquartered for the Americas in Edison, NJ with our internationally certified centers of design excellence located in the cities of Baroda, Bangalore, Chennai, Mysore, and Mumbai. IES has a global foot print with offices in the US, UK, Germany, Japan, France, Denmark, Sweden, Canada and India. 

Our parent company, Larsen & Toubro Ltd (L&T) is a technology, engineering, manufacturing and construction conglomerate, with global operations. This rich corporate heritage has given us many inherent advantages that we translate into tangible benefits for our clients. 

We have marquee clientele spanning industries such as automotive, aerospace, industrial products, medical devices, and consumer packed goods, science and technology leaders amongst others. Over 30 of our clients are Fortune 500 companies.

Specialties
Mechanical Design and Engg, Asset Information Management AIM, Embedded and Product Engg, Mobility Engg, Plant and Manufacturing Engg, Process Simulation, Lean/Six Sigma, Project Mgmnt, 3D Modeling, Product Design, Prototyping testing, Product Lifecycle Mgmnt PLM

Summary: DaVita Healthcare Partners, Inc. is one of the largest kidney care companies in the United States, with corporate headquarters in Denver, Colorado. Their offerings include in-center hemodialysis, in-center nocturnal dialysis, peritoneal dialysis, home hemodialysis, vascular access management, chronic kidney disease education, and renal diet assistance. Source: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DaVita DaVita is the dialysis division of DaVita HealthCare Partners Inc., a Fortune 500® company that, through its operating divisions, provides a variety of health care services to patient populations throughout the United States and abroad. A leading provider of kidney care in the United States, DaVita delivers dialysis services to patients with chronic kidney failure and end stage renal disease. DaVita strives to improve patients’ quality of life by innovating clinical care, and by offering integrated treatment plans, personalized care teams and convenient health-management services. As of September 30, 2012, DaVita operated or provided administrative services at 1,912 outpatient dialysis centers located in the United States serving approximately 150,000 patients. The company also operated 24 outpatient dialysis centers located in five countries outside the United States. DaVita supports numerous programs dedicated to creating positive, sustainable change in communities around the world. The company’s leadership development initiatives and social responsibility efforts have been recognized by Fortune, Modern Healthcare, Newsweek and WorldBlu. Source: http://www.davita.com/about

Summary: Amgen discovers, develops, manufactures, and delivers innovative human therapeutics. A leader in biotechnology since 1980, Amgen was one of the first companies to realize the new science’s promise by bringing safe, effective medicines from lab, to manufacturing plant, to patient. Amgen therapeutics have changed the practice of medicine, helping millions of people around the world in the fight against cancer, kidney disease, rheumatoid arthritis, bone disease, and other serious illnesses.

Amgen pioneered the development of novel products based on advances in recombinant DNA and molecular biology, and launched the biotechnology industry’s first blockbuster medicines. Today, as a Fortune 500 company serving millions of patients, Amgen continues to be an entrepreneurial, science-driven enterprise dedicated to helping people fight serious illness.

Summary: The Translational Genomics Research Institute (TGen), a non-profit 501(c)(3) organization, is focused on developing earlier diagnoses and smarter treatments. Translational genomics research employs innovative advances arising from the Human Genome Project by applying them to the development of diagnostics, prognostics, and therapies for cancer, neurological disorders, diabetes and other complex diseases. TGen's research is based on personalized medicine and the institute plans to accomplish its goals through robust and disease-focused research.

Summary: WHAT SETS BYL APART
BYL Fact Sheet
DOWNLOAD
FACT SHEET
Roll-up-our-sleeves mentality. All five of BYL’s managing partners are actively involved in the daily operation of the organization.

Training and development. Every new BYL employee undergoes an extensive on-boarding program.
Quality and consistency. BYL employs a dedicated quality control officer/auditor to monitor every aspect of the business.
Measurable results. BYL’s reporting suite puts all the cards on the table in a format that makes the most sense for each client.
Simplified software and data interfaces. A team of specialists develops and manages system integrations, data transfers, credit reporting options and custom reporting.

Summary: Pacific Edge is a New Zealand based biomedical company specialising in the discovery and commercialisation of diagnostic and prognostic technology for the early detection and monitoring of cancer.

Two products have recently completed successful clinical trials, bladder cancer detection and colorectal cancer prognosis (CRC). The bladder cancer technology Cxbladder® has been released to the market in Australia and New Zealand. CRC is being readied for commercial release. Pipeline products still in development include gastric and endometrial cancers and melanoma.

Cxbladder® is a non-invasive, accurate test that enables the early detection of bladder cancer from a small volume of urine providing general practitioners and urologists with a quick, cost effective and accurate measure of the presence of the cancer and provides urologists with the opportunity to reduce their reliance on the need for invasive tests such as cystoscopy.

We are commercialising our products from our new diagnostic laboratory in New Zealand and plan further commercialisation in the US and Europe as a centralised diagnostic service through our own facilities or as a turn-key solution, licensed to selected partners. Pacific Edge has plans for further studies in key markets to engage leading medical and urological opinion leaders who will be vital to success in new markets.

Pacific Edge:

* data accuracy
* data integrity
* fast

- centralize database?
- 50-60k tests per year in New Zealand
- US market is much bigger x10

- $800 per test fully reimbursable 
- CLIA approved lab

- fixed pricing for New Zealand market (first step: proof of concept)
- flexible/scalable for US to rapidly grow
- cheap for Chinese market (recently received patent)

Guarantee Lead position in market place (other vendors are catching up fast)

Summary: Phreesia is a patient-intake solution for doctors and patients to electronically store and enter health data. Phreesia, Inc. is a privately held company founded in 2005 by Chaim Indig and Evan Roberts. The company is based in New York City and has less than 100 employees. The company also has a sales office in Ontario, Canada. It is financially backed by HLM Venture Partners[1] and Polaris Partners.
Phreesia replaces a standard doctor's office clipboard with a PhreesiaPad – a colorful touch-screen wireless device reminiscent of an etch-a-sketch. Patients check-in using the PhreesiaPad to fill out the necessary medical records and patient history intake forms. Offices manage their patients' private health data using a back-end web portal. Phreesia offers both ad-sponsored and free educational health content on the PhreesiaPad once a patient has completed an intake form.[2][3]
Phreesia is the recipient of a 2007 DEMOgod award[4] and Chaim Indig, CEO, was named one of BusinessWeek's "Best Young Tech Entrepreneurs"[5] in April 2008.

Summary: As an industry leader, PathGroup provides comprehensive anatomic, clinical and molecular pathology services. Headquartered in Nashville, Tenn., PathGroup works with more than 70 hospitals and thousands of group practices across the United States. PathGroup is privately held and physician centered, designed to work seamlessly with medical practices and provide direct consultation when needed. We operate 24 hours a day, 7 days a week to deliver fast and accurate results, with a responsiveness that is unmatched in the industry. Our approach is keenly focused on driving better patient outcomes through high-quality, high-service diagnostics from a single point of contact. With more than 70 pathologists representing every subspecialty, PathGroup is truly One Lab providing Total Service.

Summary: naviHealth is the result of over a decade of dedication, vision, and efforts from recognized leaders and innovative organizations striving to create a leader for PAC management solutions. Our PAC program successfully reduces practice variation and improves the quality of care and patient experience. We calibrate PAC utilization by accurately predicting an individual’s recovery capabilities, the setting for PAC care, and the PAC utilization required. Accuracy is achieved by comparing each patient’s clinical characteristics to matched cases in naviHealth’s PAC database, assuring the patient achieves an optimal level of independence in the most appropriate care setting. Our data driven field-based offering improves patient outcomes while reducing PAC costs for risk-bearing healthcare organizations.
naviHealth currently provides PAC management services to over one million beneficiaries in all regions of the country through our partnerships with health plans and health systems. Our national headquarters are located in Nashville, TN.

Summary: Baptist Health Care includes four hospitals, all of which guarantee private rooms to promote better rest, limit noise and provide privacy for patients and their visitors. Additionally, the organization has two medical parks, Andrews Institute for Orthopaedic & Sports Medicine, Lakeview Center and Baptist Medical Group and Cardiology Consultants. With more than 6,400 employees and employed physicians, Baptist Health Care is the largest non-governmental employer in northwest Florida. http://www.bhcpns.org/BHC/AboutUs.aspx

Summary: The University Centers of Excellence were built to leverage the ambulatory expertise of core faculty specialists of the UCSF Fresno Medical Education Program. The faculty specialists of UCSF Fresno treat patients with state of the art practices, teach the Valley’s next generation of doctors and engage in scholarly clinical research. The Centers of Excellence enhance recruitment of new specialists to the area and develop a critical mass of physicians, surgeons and specialists to create new research and clinical fellowships for the UCSF Fresno Medical Education Program. This synergistic effect is supported by studies showing that 30 – 50% of residents and 80% of fellows remain and practice medicine in the area where they receive their training.
The University Centers of Excellence are part of Central California Faculty Medical Group (CCFMG). CCFMG is an organization of faculty physicians and staff affiliated with the prestigious University of California, San Francisco - Fresno Medical Education Program with a core purpose – To Make Medical Education Happen. All of our physicians have teaching positions with the University of California, San Francisco School of Medicine (the top medical school on the west coast and one of the top 10 nationally). They teach the next generation of physicians right here in the Central Valley at UCSF Fresno, located on the Community Regional Medical Center (CRMC) Campus in downtown. UCSF Fresno teaches approximately 200 of the best and brightest residents, who receive advanced training in emergency medicine, family practice, general surgery, internal medicine, obstetrics/gynecology, pediatrics, psychiatry and surgical critical care, all by a nationally recognized physician faculty. The UCSF Fresno campus has trained approximately one-third of the Valley physicians currently practicing in these specialty areas.
CCFMG’s primary teaching facility is CRMC (aka Community Regional). With a 58-acre campus, Community Regional brings Valley residents the highest level of care possible. It’s home to the only burn and Level I trauma centers between Los Angeles and Sacramento and a leading edge facility with cutting-edge equipment and treatment rivaling some of the top hospitals in the nation.

Summary: Today, Fountain Valley Regional Medical Center is a 400-bed, acute care facility with comprehensive services spanning from award-winning heart and stroke care, neonatal and pediatric intensive care, high-risk obstetrical services, bariatric surgery, and orthopedics, spine and neurosurgery.

As one of the largest hospitals in Orange County and the largest employer in Fountain Valley, we're proud of our heritage as a caring, community hospital that strives to meet the needs of our neighbors by providing specialized services, skilled and caring physicians and staff, and a high level of quality care.

Summary: GAIA Software, founded in 1998, is an industry leader in providing dialysis facility management tools and consulting services. Our products enable caregivers and managers to offer the highest quality care while maintaining control over the business side of dialysis.
Founder and President Bruce J. Thompson serves on the Board of Directors of the NRAA. He often speaks at conventions and has contributed to leading industry magazines. Mr. Thompson’s experience also includes several years spent as the CFO of a dialysis facility. His industry leadership and vision help guide GAIA Software to produce state of the art products that meet the needs of today’s dialysis facilities.
Our philosophy is to take a holistic view of the dialysis business in order to provide products that are integrated, easy to use, and focused on caregivers. In addition, we believe in supporting our customers with frequent, relevant updates and personal service.
